title: Shivneri Fort
tagline: Shivneri Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Shivneri Fort (known as Killa) (Marathi pronunciation: [ʃiʋneɾiː]) is an ancient military fortification located near Junnar in Pune district in Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Shivneri Fort (known as Killa) (Marathi pronunciation: [ʃiʋneɾiː]) is an ancient military fortification located near Junnar in Pune district in Maharashtra, India. It is the birthplace of Chatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, the founder of Maratha Kingdom.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Shivneri Caves
topPlaces.description: The Shivneri Caves are artificial caves dug for Buddhist monks circa the 1st century CE. These are now famous tourist attractions located on Shivneri Hill, about 2 km Southwest of Junnar, India. Other caves around the city of Junnar are: Manmodi Caves, Lenyadri, and the Tulja Caves.

topPlaces.name: Junnar
topPlaces.description: Junnar (Marathi: [d͡ʒunːəɾ]) is a city in the Pune district of the Indian state of Maharashtra. The city has history dating back to the first millennium.

topPlaces.name: Shankarrao Butte Patil Vidyalaya, Junnar
topPlaces.description: Shankarrao Butte Patil Vidyalaya, formerly New English School, Junnar is one of the oldest and reputed school in the western Maharashtra, in the town of Junnar in Pune district. It was established in 1935 by the Junnar Education Society under the intellectual and academic leadership of the famous educator R.P.Sabnis, a Cambridge University alumni.

topPlaces.name: Alitalia Flight 771
topPlaces.description: Alitalia Flight 771 was a multi-leg Douglas DC-8-43 international scheduled flight from Sydney via Darwin, Bangkok, Bombay, Karachi, and Tehran to Rome with 94 on board. On 7 July 1962 18:40 UTC (8 July 1962, 00:10 local) it crashed into a hill about 84 kilometres (52 mi) north-east of Bombay while on approach.

topPlaces.name: Tulja Caves
topPlaces.description: Tulja Caves (Tulja Lena) are located beyond the Shivneri hill, about 4 km to the west of Junnar, India. Other caves surrounding the city of Junnar are: Manmodi Caves, Shivneri Caves and Lenyadri caves.

topPlaces.name: Manmodi Caves
topPlaces.description: The Manmodi Caves are a complex of a rock-cut caves about 3 km to the south of the city of Junnar in India. Other caves surrounding the city of Junnar are: Tulja Caves, Shivneri Caves and Lenyadri caves.

topPlaces.name: Dhamankhel
topPlaces.description: Dhamankhel is a village situated at about 3 km from Junnar city of Pune district. is a village located approximately 3 kilometres from Junnarcity in the Pune district of Maharashtra, India.

topPlaces.name: Habsi Gumbaz
topPlaces.description: Habsi Gumbaz or Saudagar Gumbaz is a tomb located on the outskirts of Junnar. It is listed as a monument of national importance.

items.note: The Shivneri Caves are artificial caves dug for Buddhist monks circa the 1st century CE. These are now famous tourist attractions located on Shivneri Hill, about 2 km Southwest of Junnar, India. Other caves around the city of Junnar are: Manmodi Caves, Lenyadri, and the Tulja Caves.
Junnar (Marathi: [d͡ʒunːəɾ]) is a city in the Pune district of the Indian state of Maharashtra. The city has history dating back to the first millennium.

Shankarrao Butte Patil Vidyalaya, formerly New English School, Junnar is one of the oldest and reputed school in the western Maharashtra, in the town of Junnar in Pune district. It was established in 1935 by the Junnar Education Society under the intellectual and academic leadership of the famous educator R.P.Sabnis, a Cambridge University alumni.

Alitalia Flight 771 was a multi-leg Douglas DC-8-43 international scheduled flight from Sydney via Darwin, Bangkok, Bombay, Karachi, and Tehran to Rome with 94 on board. On 7 July 1962 18:40 UTC (8 July 1962, 00:10 local) it crashed into a hill about 84 kilometres (52 mi) north-east of Bombay while on approach.

Tulja Caves (Tulja Lena) are located beyond the Shivneri hill, about 4 km to the west of Junnar, India. Other caves surrounding the city of Junnar are: Manmodi Caves, Shivneri Caves and Lenyadri caves.

The Manmodi Caves are a complex of a rock-cut caves about 3 km to the south of the city of Junnar in India. Other caves surrounding the city of Junnar are: Tulja Caves, Shivneri Caves and Lenyadri caves.
Dhamankhel is a village situated at about 3 km from Junnar city of Pune district. is a village located approximately 3 kilometres from Junnarcity in the Pune district of Maharashtra, India.
Habsi Gumbaz or Saudagar Gumbaz is a tomb located on the outskirts of Junnar. It is listed as a monument of national importance.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Shivneri Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Shivneri Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Shivneri Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pune Airport (69 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (74 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Shivneri Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Shivneri Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Shivneri Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Shivneri Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Shivneri Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Shivneri Caves, Junnar, Shankarrao Butte Patil Vidyalaya, Junnar, Alitalia Flight 771, Tulja Caves.
